turns.content: Good afternoon, and welcome to the Microsoft Corp. Q1 fiscal year 2024 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

This was a challenging quarter as we navigated headwinds across several of our markets. In Q1 2024, Revenue declined 13% year over year to $43.1B, impacted by softening demand in key segments. Our AI platform strategy continues to gain traction, with Microsoft 365 showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in AI platform because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from cloud infrastructure validate our approach. We expect near-term headwinds to persist but believe our strategic positioning will drive recovery.

Thank you, Satya. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q1 2024 was $92.5 billion, representing a decline of 6% year over year. Our cloud revenue reached $26.2 billion, consistent with our expectations. Gross margin was 45.7%, benefiting from mix shift and operational efficiencies. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $24.9 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $14 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For Azure, we saw 10% growth, driven by enterprise adoption.
Great, thanks. On Azure, can you talk about what you are seeing in terms of customer adoption and how the competitive landscape is evolving?

Sure, Keith. The Azure growth rate trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, we are working to stabilize them and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the cloud revenue trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Thanks, Raimo. Great question. On cloud revenue, while the current environment presents challenges, we are taking proactive steps to position LinkedIn for recovery. We are focused on operational efficiency and protecting our long-term competitive position. We expect LinkedIn to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Amy for the specific numbers.
I wanted to ask about enterprise transformation. How much of your capital allocation is going toward this area, and when do you expect it to start contributing meaningfully to the top line?

Sure, Amit. The operating margin trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, we are working to stabilize them and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
My question is on the demand environment. Are you seeing any changes in customer behavior or deal cycles compared to last quarter, particularly in Azure?

Thanks, Erik. Great question. On Azure growth rate, while the current environment presents challenges, we are taking proactive steps to position Azure for recovery. We are focused on operational efficiency and protecting our long-term competitive position. We expect Azure to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Amy for the specific numbers.

Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the Azure growth rate trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Sure, Shannon. The Azure growth rate trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, we are working to stabilize them and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

Good afternoon, and welcome to the Microsoft Corp. Q2 fiscal year 2024 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

We delivered solid results this quarter, broadly in line with our expectations. In Q2 2024, operating margin was largely stable compared to the prior quarter, reflecting balanced supply and demand dynamics. Our capital expenditure strategy continues to gain traction, with GitHub Copilot showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in capital expenditure because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from cloud infrastructure validate our approach. For the upcoming quarter, we expect results roughly in line with seasonal patterns.

Thank you, Satya. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q2 2024 was $39.5 billion, representing growth of 30% year over year. Our cloud revenue reached $49.6 billion, consistent with our expectations. Gross margin expanded 29.3%, reflecting our continued investment in growth. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $27.8 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $5 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For Azure, we saw 14% growth, driven by enterprise adoption.
Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the cloud revenue trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Sure, Mark. The cloud revenue trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.

Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the operating margin trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Thanks, Kash. Great question. On operating margin, we are pleased with the progress on Copilot adoption and continue to see steady adoption. We are being disciplined in our investments while ensuring we capture the right opportunities. We expect Dynamics to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Amy for the specific numbers.

Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the operating margin trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Thanks, Shannon. Great question. On operating margin, we are pleased with the progress on Copilot adoption and continue to see steady adoption. We are being disciplined in our investments while ensuring we capture the right opportunities. We expect GitHub Copilot to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Amy for the specific numbers.

Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the operating margin trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Sure, Brent. The operating margin trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

Good afternoon, and welcome to the Microsoft Corp. Q3 fiscal year 2024 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

I am extremely pleased with our performance this quarter. The momentum we are seeing is unprecedented. In Q3 2024, We saw 18% growth in GitHub Copilot, well above our internal targets. Our enterprise transformation strategy continues to gain traction, with GitHub Copilot showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in enterprise transformation because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from cloud infrastructure validate our approach. Our pipeline is the strongest it has ever been, and we are raising our full-year guidance accordingly.

Thank you, Satya. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q3 2024 was $37.6 billion, representing growth of 14% year over year. Our cloud revenue reached $34.4 billion, accelerating the strong demand trends we have been seeing. Gross margin expanded 38.8%, benefiting from mix shift and operational efficiencies. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $29.6 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $8 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For Azure, we saw 10% growth, driven by enterprise adoption.
I wanted to ask about Copilot adoption. How much of your capital allocation is going toward this area, and when do you expect it to start contributing meaningfully to the top line?

Sure, Brad. The Azure growth rate trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
I wanted to ask about enterprise transformation. How much of your capital allocation is going toward this area, and when do you expect it to start contributing meaningfully to the top line?

Sure, Mark. The operating margin trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
Great, thanks. On LinkedIn, can you talk about what you are seeing in terms of customer adoption and how the competitive landscape is evolving?

Thanks, Karl. Great question. On Azure growth rate, we are seeing exceptionally strong demand and our conviction in Copilot adoption has only strengthened. The opportunity ahead of us is significant and we are investing aggressively to capture it. We expect LinkedIn to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Amy for the specific numbers.

Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the commercial bookings trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Sure, Wamsi. The commercial bookings trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

I wanted to ask about Copilot adoption. How much of your capital allocation is going toward this area, and when do you expect it to start contributing meaningfully to the top line?

Thanks, Amit. Great question. On Azure growth rate, we are seeing exceptionally strong demand and our conviction in Copilot adoption has only strengthened. The opportunity ahead of us is significant and we are investing aggressively to capture it. We expect GitHub Copilot to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Amy for the specific numbers.
I wanted to ask about capital expenditure. How much of your capital allocation is going toward this area, and when do you expect it to start contributing meaningfully to the top line?

Sure, Brad. The commercial bookings trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
I wanted to ask about enterprise transformation. How much of your capital allocation is going toward this area, and when do you expect it to start contributing meaningfully to the top line?

Sure, Erik. The Azure growth rate trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

Good afternoon, and welcome to the Microsoft Corp. Q1 fiscal year 2025 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

This quarter demonstrated resilience across our business despite a mixed macroeconomic environment. In Q1 2025, We achieved 16% growth in Dynamics, which is in the range we expected given current market conditions. Our Copilot adoption strategy continues to gain traction, with Dynamics showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in Copilot adoption because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from cloud infrastructure validate our approach. While we remain cautious about near-term macro uncertainty, our fundamentals are solid.

Thank you, Satya. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q1 2025 was $38.7 billion, representing growth of 20% year over year. Our cloud revenue reached $43.2 billion, consistent with our expectations. Gross margin expanded 26.2%, reflecting our continued investment in growth. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated $22.6 billion in free cash flow during the quarter. We returned $12 billion to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. For Azure, we saw 17% growth, driven by enterprise adoption.
Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the cloud revenue trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Sure, Brent. The cloud revenue trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.

Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the operating margin trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Sure, Karl. The operating margin trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, they continue to improve and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
Great, thanks. On Azure, can you talk about what you are seeing in terms of customer adoption and how the competitive landscape is evolving?

Thanks, Raimo. Great question. On commercial bookings, we are pleased with the progress on capital expenditure and continue to see steady adoption. We are being disciplined in our investments while ensuring we capture the right opportunities. We expect Azure to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Amy for the specific numbers.

Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the operating margin trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Thanks, Keith. Great question. On operating margin, we are pleased with the progress on Copilot adoption and continue to see steady adoption. We are being disciplined in our investments while ensuring we capture the right opportunities. We expect Microsoft 365 to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Amy for the specific numbers.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.

Good afternoon, and welcome to the Microsoft Corp. Q3 fiscal year 2025 earnings conference call. All lines have been placed on mute to prevent background noise. After the speakers' remarks, there will be a question and answer session. I would now like to turn the call over to the head of investor relations.

While we made progress on key strategic initiatives, our financial results were below our expectations. In Q3 2025, Azure growth rate came in below expectations, reflecting ongoing challenges in the current environment. Our Copilot adoption strategy continues to gain traction, with Azure showing particularly strong adoption among enterprise customers. We are investing significantly in Copilot adoption because we believe this represents a generational opportunity. The early results from enterprise solutions validate our approach. We expect near-term headwinds to persist but believe our strategic positioning will drive recovery.

Thank you, Satya. Let me walk through the financial details. Total revenue for Q3 2025 was $59.8 billion, representing a decline of 7% year over year. Our cloud revenue reached $33.3 billion, consistent with our expectations. Gross margin was 23.4%, reflecting our continued investment in growth. For Azure, we saw 19% growth, driven by enterprise adoption.

Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the commercial bookings trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Sure, Erik. The commercial bookings trend reflects the underlying strength in our business model. When you look at the unit economics, we are working to stabilize them and that gives us confidence in the sustainability of these results.
Can you help us understand the margin dynamics better? What is driving the pressure and how should we model this going forward?

Thanks, Brent. Great question. On cloud revenue, while the current environment presents challenges, we are taking proactive steps to position Dynamics for recovery. We are focused on operational efficiency and protecting our long-term competitive position. We expect Dynamics to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Amy for the specific numbers.

Thank you for taking my question. Can you provide more color on the commercial bookings trajectory? Specifically, how should we think about the sustainability of the growth rate you reported this quarter?

Thanks, Amit. Great question. On commercial bookings, while the current environment presents challenges, we are taking proactive steps to position Azure for recovery. We are focused on operational efficiency and protecting our long-term competitive position. We expect Azure to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Amy for the specific numbers.
My question is on the demand environment. Are you seeing any changes in customer behavior or deal cycles compared to last quarter, particularly in GitHub Copilot?

Thanks, Karl. Great question. On Azure growth rate, while the current environment presents challenges, we are taking proactive steps to position GitHub Copilot for recovery. We are focused on operational efficiency and protecting our long-term competitive position. We expect GitHub Copilot to continue to be a meaningful growth driver as we move through the rest of the year. Let me hand it to Amy for the specific numbers.
This concludes the question and answer session. I would like to turn the call back to management for closing remarks.
Thank you all for joining us today. We are excited about the opportunities ahead and look forward to updating you on our progress next quarter. Have a great evening.
